steps.title: Identify what the answer must do
steps.content: The blank completes "suggesting that ______". Peter Blair Henry found that companies in 11 liberalizing countries received significant investment increases in the three years following liberalization. The economists' standard view was that liberalization makes capital-raising easier. Any valid inference must follow from what Henry's data actually showed.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — companies in Malaysia experienced a greater increase in investment following liberalization than did companies in the Philippines.: The passage gives Henry's findings as an average across 11 countries. No country-by-country comparison is made or implied. ✗
  • Choice B — economists who held the standard view of liberalization failed to anticipate some serious negative effects of liberalization.: Henry's findings were positive — significant investment increases. The passage mentions no negative effects. This directly contradicts the data. ✗
  • Choice C — companies did not benefit from liberalization until at least three years after liberalization occurred.: The passage says benefits appeared in the three years following liberalization, meaning they began within that window, not that they were delayed until after three years. This misreads the timeline. ✗
  • Choice D — empirical evidence was consistent with the scholarly consensus about the consequences of liberalization.: The scholarly consensus was that liberalization helps companies raise capital. Henry's empirical finding of significant investment increases confirms that consensus. ✓
D is correct.
